title: Sarga 26
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, mind and meditation, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “There, the powerful Ravana, along with his army, chose to reside as the sun was setting.” through “Where they sang together in unison, their songs greatly increased the satisfaction of the mind.” through “The night had set in, enhanced by the melodious music, the profusion of flowers, the cool mountain breeze, and the rising of the moon.”

Themes: self-knowledge, mind and meditation, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “Ravana, despite his great might, had fallen under the sway of desire and repeatedly sighed deeply as he gazed at the moon.” through “Her face was like the moon, her beautiful eyebrows like bows, her thighs like elephant trunks, and her hands soft as new leaves; as she walked…” through “Who today will ascend your broad hips, which resemble a golden disc, are adorned with golden chains, and are like heaven itself?”

Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, virtue and right conduct. The passage moves from “Who is that man superior to me today, be it Indra, Vishnu, or the Ashvins, whom you, O timid one, would go to, leaving me behind, for that would…” through “Then Ravana spoke to her, who was standing with her face turned downwards towards his feet, having gotten goosebumps merely upon seeing him.” through “Thus, my affection is indeed for him alone, and not for anyone else.”

Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, virtue and right conduct. The passage moves from “O King, destroyer of enemies, by that truth, you ought to release me.” through “Rambha, thus released, with her garlands and ornaments fallen, became agitated like a river churned by the sport of a lordly elephant.” through “O god, this Dashagriva arrived here to go to heaven, and with the help of his army, he spent this night.”

Themes: devotion, mind and meditation, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “O destroyer of enemies, I was seen by him while coming to your presence, and I was seized and asked by that Rakshasa, "Whose are you?"” through “Having taken the water and performed the ritual purification, he then uttered a terrible curse upon the king of Rakshasas (Ravana).” through “But having heard that terrifying, hair-raising curse, Dashagriva no longer desired sexual intimacy with unwilling women.”
All the chaste women who had been abducted by him attained great joy upon hearing that delightful curse uttered by Nalakubara. इत्यार्षे…

verses.sanskrit: स तु तत्र दशग्रीवः सह सैन्येन वीर्यवान् । अस्तं प्राप्ते दिनकरे निवासं समरोचयत् ।। 7.26.1 ।।
verses.translation: There, the powerful Ravana, along with his army, chose to reside as the sun was setting.

verses.sanskrit: उदिते विमले चन्द्रे तुल्यपर्वतवर्चसि । प्रसुप्तं सुमहत्सैन्यं नानाप्रहरणायुधम् ।। 7.26.2 ।।
verses.translation: As the pure moon, shining with the brilliance of a mountain, rose, a vast army equipped with diverse weapons lay asleep.

verses.sanskrit: रावणस्तु महावीर्यो निषण्णः शैलमूर्धनि । स ददर्श गुणांस्तत्र चन्द्रपादपशोभितान् ।। 7.26.3 ।।
verses.translation: But the mighty Ravana, seated on the mountain peak, beheld the beautiful features of that place, enhanced by trees illuminated by the moonlight.

verses.sanskrit: कर्णिकारवनैर्दीप्तैः कदम्बगहनैस्तथा । पद्मिनीभिश्च फुल्लाभिर्मन्दाकिन्या जलैरपि ।। 7.26.4 ।।
verses.translation: He saw the beauty created by radiant Karnikara forests, dense Kadamba groves, blooming lotus ponds, and the waters of the Mandakini river.

verses.sanskrit: चम्पकाशोकपुन्नागमन्दारतरुभिस्तथा । चूतपाटललोध्रैश्च प्रियङ्ग्वर्जुनकेतकैः । तगरैर्नारिकैलैश्च प्रियालपनसैस्तथा ।। 7.26.5 ।।
verses.translation: The scene was further adorned by trees such as Champaka, Ashoka, Punnaga, Mandara, Mango, Patala, Lodhra, Priyangu, Arjuna, Ketaka, Tagara, Narikela, Priyala, and Panasa.

verses.sanskrit: आरग्वधैस्तमालैश्च प्रियालवकुलैरपि । एतैरन्यैश्च तरुभिरुद्भासितवनान्तरे । किन्नरा मदनेनार्ता रक्ता मधुरकण्ठिनः ।। 7.26.6 ।।
verses.translation: In that forest region, illuminated by these and other trees like Aragvadha, Tamala, Priyala, and Bakula, Kinnaras, tormented by love, sang passionately with their sweet voices.

verses.sanskrit: समं सम्प्रजगुर्यत्र मनस्तुष्टिविवर्धनम् ।। 7.26.7 ।।
verses.translation: Where they sang together in unison, their songs greatly increased the satisfaction of the mind.

verses.sanskrit: भिरुद्भासितवनान्तरे । विद्याधरा मदक्षीबा मदरक्तान्तलोचनाः । योषिद्भिः सह सङ्क्रान्ताश्चिक्रीडुर्जहृषुश्च वै ।। 7.26.8 ।।
verses.translation: In the brightly lit forest interior, the Vidyadharas, intoxicated and with eyes red from drink, assembled with their women and indeed played and rejoiced.

verses.sanskrit: घण्टानामिव सन्नादः शुश्रुवे मधुरस्वरः । अप्सरोगणसङ्घानां गायतां धनदालये ।। 7.26.9 ।।
verses.translation: In Kubera's abode, a sweet sound was heard, like the ringing of bells, from the singing groups of Apsaras.

verses.sanskrit: पुष्पवर्षाणि मुञ्चन्तो नगाः पवनताडिताः । शैलं तं वासयन्तीव मधुमाधवगन्धिनः ।। 7.26.10 ।।
verses.translation: The trees, swayed by the wind, released showers of flowers, as if perfuming that mountain with the fragrance of spring blossoms.

verses.sanskrit: मधुपुष्परजःपृक्तं गन्धमादाय पुष्कलम् । प्रववौ वर्धयन्कामं रावणस्य सुखो ऽनिलः ।। 7.26.11 ।।
verses.translation: A pleasant wind blew, carrying the abundant fragrance mixed with the pollen of spring flowers, thereby increasing Ravana's desire.

verses.sanskrit: गेयात्पुष्पसमृद्ध्या च शैत्याद्वायोर्गिरेर्गुणात् । प्रवृत्तायां रजन्यां च चन्द्रस्योदयनेन च ।। 7.26.12 ।।
verses.translation: The night had set in, enhanced by the melodious music, the profusion of flowers, the cool mountain breeze, and the rising of the moon.

verses.sanskrit: रावणस्तु महावीर्यः कामस्य वशमागतः । विनिःश्वस्य विनिःश्वस्य शशिनं समवैक्षत ।। 7.26.13 ।।
verses.translation: Ravana, despite his great might, had fallen under the sway of desire and repeatedly sighed deeply as he gazed at the moon.

verses.sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नन्तरे तत्र दिव्याभरणभूषिता । सर्वाप्सरोवरा रम्भा दिव्यपुष्पविभूषिता ।। 7.26.14 ।।
verses.translation: At that very moment, Rambha, the foremost of all Apsaras, appeared there, adorned with divine ornaments and divine flowers.

verses.sanskrit: दिव्यचन्दनलिप्ताङ्गी मन्दारकृतमूर्धजा । दिव्योत्सवकृतारम्भा पूर्णचन्द्रनिभानना ।। 7.26.15 ।।
verses.translation: Her body was smeared with divine sandalwood, her hair adorned with Mandara flowers, she had initiated a divine celebration, and her face shone like the full moon.

verses.sanskrit: चक्षुर्मनोहरं पीनं मेखलादामभूषितम् । समुद्वहन्ती जघनं रतिप्राभृतमुत्तमम् ।। 7.26.16 ।।
verses.translation: She bore her hips, which were plump, charming to the eyes and mind, adorned with a girdle of bells, and an excellent offering for love.

verses.sanskrit: कृतैर्विशेषकैरार्द्रैः षडर्तुकुसुमोद्भवैः ।। 7.26.17 ।।
verses.translation: She was adorned with fresh, special marks made from flowers of all six seasons.

verses.sanskrit: बभावन्यतमेव श्रीकान्तिद्युतिमतिह्रियाम् । नीलं सतोयमेघाभं वस्त्रं समवकुण्ठिता ।। 7.26.18 ।।
verses.translation: She appeared as if another embodiment of beauty, splendor, radiance, intelligence, and modesty, covered in a blue garment resembling a water-laden cloud.

verses.sanskrit: यस्या वक्त्रं शशिनिभं भ्रुवौ चापनिभे शुभे । ऊरू करिकराकारौ करौ पल्लवकोमलौ । सैन्यमध्येन गच्छन्ती रावणेनोपवीक्षिता ।। 7.26.19 ।।
verses.translation: Her face was like the moon, her beautiful eyebrows like bows, her thighs like elephant trunks, and her hands soft as new leaves; as she walked through the army, she was observed by Ravana.

verses.sanskrit: तां समुत्थाय गच्छन्तीं कामबाणवशं गतः । करे गृहीत्वा लज्जन्तीं स्मयमानो ऽभ्यभाषत ।। 7.26.20 ।।
verses.translation: Having risen, Ravana, fallen under the sway of Cupid's arrows, seized her hand as she was going, and smiling, spoke to the blushing woman.

verses.sanskrit: क्व गच्छसि वरारोहे कां सिद्धिं भजसे स्वयम् । कस्याभ्युदयकालो ऽयं यस्त्वां समुपभोक्ष्यते ।। 7.26.21 ।।
verses.translation: "Where do you go, O lady of beautiful hips, what accomplishment do you seek for yourself, and whose time of prosperity is this, that he will enjoy you?"

verses.sanskrit: त्वदाननरसस्याद्य पद्मोत्पलसुगन्धिनः । सुधामृतरसस्येव को ऽद्य तृप्तिं गमिष्यति ।। 7.26.22 ।।
verses.translation: Who today will ever be satisfied with the essence of your face, fragrant like lotuses and water-lilies, which is like the essence of divine nectar?

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्णकुम्भनिभौ पीनौ शुभौ भीरु निरन्तरौ । कस्योरस्थलसंस्पर्शं दास्यतस्ते कुचाविमौ ।। 7.26.23 ।।
verses.translation: O timid one, whose chest will these two plump, beautiful, close-set breasts of yours, resembling golden pitchers, touch?

verses.sanskrit: सुवर्णचक्रप्रतिमं स्वर्णदामचितं पृथु । अध्यारोहति कस्ते ऽद्य जघनं स्वर्गरूपिणम् ।। 7.26.24 ।।
verses.translation: Who today will ascend your broad hips, which resemble a golden disc, are adorned with golden chains, and are like heaven itself?

verses.sanskrit: मद्विशिष्टः पुमान्को ऽद्य शक्रो विष्णुरथाश्विनौ । मामतीत्य हि यं च त्वं यासि भीरु न शोभनम् ।। 7.26.25 ।।
verses.translation: Who is that man superior to me today, be it Indra, Vishnu, or the Ashvins, whom you, O timid one, would go to, leaving me behind, for that would not be proper?

verses.sanskrit: विश्रम त्वं पृथुश्रोणि शिलातलमिदं शुभम् ।। 7.26.26 ।।
verses.translation: O broad-hipped one, you should rest on this beautiful rock surface.

verses.sanskrit: त्रैलोक्ये यः प्रभुश्चैव मदन्यो नैव विद्यते ।। 7.26.27 ।।
verses.translation: There is no lord in the three worlds other than me.

verses.sanskrit: तदेवं प्राञ्जलिः प्रह्वो याचते त्वां दशाननः । भर्तुर्भर्ता विधाता च त्रैलोक्यस्य भजस्व माम् ।। 7.26.28 ।।
verses.translation: Therefore, the ten-headed Ravana, bowing down with folded hands, begs you, "Worship me, for I am the lord of lords and the creator of the three worlds."

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्ता ऽब्रवीद्रम्भा वेपमाना कृताञ्जलिः । प्रसीद नार्हसे वक्तुमीदृशं त्वं हि मे गुरुः ।। 7.26.29 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed, Rambha, trembling and with folded hands, replied, "Please have mercy; you should not speak such words, for you are indeed like an elder to me."

verses.sanskrit: अन्येभ्यो हि त्वया रक्ष्या प्राप्नुयां धर्षणं यदि । तद्धर्मतः स्नुषा ते ऽहं तत्त्वमेव ब्रवीमि ते ।। 7.26.30 ।।
verses.translation: You are indeed the one who should protect me from molestation by others, and by dharma, I am your daughter-in-law; I tell you this truth.

verses.sanskrit: अथाब्रवीद्दशग्रीवश्चरणाधोमुखीं स्थिताम् । रोमहर्षमनुप्राप्तां दृष्टमात्रेण तां तदा ।। 7.26.31 ।।
verses.translation: Then Ravana spoke to her, who was standing with her face turned downwards towards his feet, having gotten goosebumps merely upon seeing him.

verses.sanskrit: सुतस्य यदि मे भार्या ततस्त्वं हि स्नुषा भवेः । बाढमित्येव सा रम्भा प्राह रावणमुत्तरम् ।। 7.26.32 ।।
verses.translation: Rambha replied to Ravana, saying, "If I were the wife of your son, then you would indeed be my father-in-law."

verses.sanskrit: धर्मतस्ते सुतस्याहं भार्या राक्षसपुङ्गव । पुत्रः प्रियतरः प्राणैर्भ्रातुर्वैश्रवणस्य ते । विख्यातस्त्रिषु लोकेषु नलकूबर इत्ययम् ।। 7.26.33 ।।
verses.translation: O best of Rakshasas, I am rightfully the wife of your son, Nalakubara, who is dearer to your brother Vaishravana (Kubera) than life itself and is renowned in all three worlds.

verses.sanskrit: धर्मतो यो भवेद्विप्रः क्षत्रियो वीर्यतो भवेत् । क्रोधाद्यश्च भवेदग्निः क्षान्त्या च वसुधासमः ।। 7.26.34 ।।
verses.translation: He (Nalakubara) is like a Brahmin by righteousness, a Kshatriya by valor, fire by his anger, and equal to the earth in patience.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यास्मि कृतसङ्केता लोकपालसुतस्य वै । तमुद्दिश्य तु मे सर्वं विभूषणमिदं कृतम् ।। 7.26.35 ।।
verses.translation: I am indeed promised to him, the son of a Lokapala, and all these ornaments I wear are for his sake.

verses.sanskrit: तथा तस्य हि नान्यस्य भावो मां प्रति तिष्ठति ।। 7.26.36 ।।
verses.translation: Thus, my affection is indeed for him alone, and not for anyone else.

verses.sanskrit: तेन सत्येन मां राजन्मोक्तुमर्हस्यरिन्दम ।। 7.26.37 ।।
verses.translation: O King, destroyer of enemies, by that truth, you ought to release me.

verses.sanskrit: स हि तिष्ठति धर्मात्मा मां प्रतीक्ष्य समुत्सुकः । तत्र विघ्नं सुतस्येह कर्तुं नार्हसि मुञ्च माम् ।। 7.26.38 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, that righteous one waits for me, very eager; you ought not to create an obstacle for your son in this matter, so release me.

verses.sanskrit: सद्भिराचरितं मार्गं गच्छ राक्षसुपुङ्गव । माननीयो मम त्वं हि पालनीया तथा ऽस्मि ते ।। 7.26.39 ।।
verses.translation: O best of Rakshasas, follow the path practiced by the good; you are indeed worthy of respect to me, and I am similarly to be protected by you.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्तो दशग्रीवः प्रत्युवाच विनीतवत् । स्नुषा ऽस्मि यदवोचस्त्वमेकपत्नीष्वयं क्रमः । देवलोकस्थितिरियं सुराणां शाश्वती मता ।। 7.26.40 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed, Dashagriva replied as if humbly, saying that what she spoke about being a daughter-in-law and the custom of having one husband applies only to women with a single spouse, and that this custom of the divine world for gods is considered eternal.

verses.sanskrit: पतिरप्सरसां नास्ति न चैकस्त्रीपरिग्रहः ।। 7.26.41 ।।
verses.translation: There is no single husband for Apsaras, nor is there a custom of having only one wife.

verses.sanskrit: अस्मि यदवोचस्त्वमेकपत्नीष्वयं क्रमः । एवमुक्त्वा स तां रक्षो निवेश्य च शिलातले । कामभोगाभिसंसक्तो मैथुनायोपचक्रमे ।। 7.26.42 ।।
verses.translation: Having said, "I am what you said, this is the rule among chaste wives," the demon Ravana, deeply engrossed in sensual pleasures, placed her on a rock surface and proceeded to engage in sexual union.

verses.sanskrit: सा विमुक्ता ततो रम्भा भ्रष्टमाल्यविभूषणा । गजेन्द्राक्रीडमथिता नदीवाकुलतां गता ।। 7.26.43 ।।
verses.translation: Rambha, thus released, with her garlands and ornaments fallen, became agitated like a river churned by the sport of a lordly elephant.

verses.sanskrit: लुलिताकुलकेशान्ता करवेपितपल्लवा । पवनेनावधूतेव लता कुसुमशालिनी ।। 7.26.44 ।।
verses.translation: Her hair was disheveled and scattered, and her hands trembled like sprouts, resembling a flower-laden creeper shaken by the wind.

verses.sanskrit: सा वेपमाना लज्जन्ती भीता करकृताञ्जलिः । नलकूबरमासाद्य पादयोर्निपपात ह ।। 7.26.45 ।।
verses.translation: Trembling, ashamed, and frightened, she approached Nalakubara with folded hands and fell at his feet.

verses.sanskrit: तदवस्थां च तां दृष्ट्वा महात्मा नलकूबरः । अब्रवीत्किमिदं भद्रे पादयोः पतिता ऽसि मे ।। 7.26.46 ।।
verses.translation: Seeing her in that state, the great-souled Nalakubara asked, "O gentle one, what is this, that you have fallen at my feet?"

verses.sanskrit: सा वै निःश्वसमाना तु वेपमाना कृताञ्जलिः । तस्मै सर्वं यथातत्त्वमाख्यातुमुपचक्रमे ।। 7.26.47 ।।
verses.translation: Trembling and sighing deeply, she, with folded hands, began to narrate everything to him exactly as it had happened.

verses.sanskrit: एष देव दशग्रीवः प्राप्तो गन्तुं त्रिविष्टपम् । तेन सैन्यसहायेन निशेयं परिणामिता ।। 7.26.48 ।।
verses.translation: O god, this Dashagriva arrived here to go to heaven, and with the help of his army, he spent this night.

verses.sanskrit: आयन्ती तेन दृष्टा ऽस्मि त्वत्सकाशमरिन्दम । गृहीता तेन पृष्टा ऽस्मि कस्य त्वमिति रक्षसा ।। 7.26.49 ।।
verses.translation: O destroyer of enemies, I was seen by him while coming to your presence, and I was seized and asked by that Rakshasa, "Whose are you?"

verses.sanskrit: मया तु सर्वं यत्सत्यं तस्मै सर्वं निवेदितम् । काममोहाभिभूतात्मा नाश्रौषीत्तद्वचो मम ।। 7.26.50 ।।
verses.translation: But I reported everything truly to him, yet his mind, overcome by lust and delusion, did not listen to my words.

verses.sanskrit: याच्यमानो मया देव स्नुषा ते ऽहमिति प्रभो । तत्सर्वं पृष्ठतः कृत्वा बलात्तेनास्मि धर्षिता ।। 7.26.51 ।।
verses.translation: O god, O lord, despite being implored by me that I am your daughter-in-law, he disregarded all that and forcibly violated me.

verses.sanskrit: एवं त्वमपराधं मे क्षन्तुमर्हसि सुव्रत । न हि तुल्यं बलं सौम्य स्त्रियाश्च पुरुषस्य च ।। 7.26.52 ।।
verses.translation: O virtuous and gentle one, you ought to forgive my offense, for indeed, the strength of a woman and a man is not equal.

verses.sanskrit: एतछुत्वा तु सङ्क्रुद्धस्तदा वैश्रवणात्मजः । धर्षणां तां परां श्रुत्वा ध्यानं सम्प्रविवेश ह ।। 7.26.53 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard this, the son of Vaishravana (Kubera) became greatly enraged and, upon hearing of that extreme outrage, he entered into deep contemplation.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य तत्कर्म विज्ञाय तदा वैश्रवणात्मजः । मुहूर्तात्क्रोधताम्राक्षस्तोयं जग्राह पाणिना ।। 7.26.54 ।।
verses.translation: Having understood that deed of his, the son of Vaishravana (Kubera) instantly, with eyes red from anger, took water in his hand.

verses.sanskrit: गृहीत्वा सलिलं सर्वमुपस्पृश्य यथाविधि । उत्ससर्ज यथा शापं राक्षसेन्द्राय दारुणम् ।। 7.26.55 ।।
verses.translation: Having taken the water and performed the ritual purification, he then uttered a terrible curse upon the king of Rakshasas (Ravana).

verses.sanskrit: अकामा तेन यस्मात्त्वं बलाद्भद्रे प्रधर्षिता । तस्मात्स युवतीमन्यां नाकामामुपयास्यति ।। 7.26.56 ।।
verses.translation: O auspicious one, because you were molested by him by force against your will, therefore he will not approach another young woman who is unwilling.

verses.sanskrit: यदा ह्यकामां कामार्तो धर्षयिष्यति योषितम् । मूर्धा तु सप्तधा तस्य शकलीभविता तदा ।। 7.26.57 ।।
verses.translation: When a lust-struck man violates an unwilling woman, his head will indeed shatter into seven pieces.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिन्नुदाहृते शापे ज्वलिताग्निसमप्रभे । देवदुन्दुभयो नेदुः पुष्पवृष्टिश्च खाच्च्युता । पितामहमुखाश्चैव सर्वे देवाः प्रहर्षिताः ।। 7.26.58 ।।
verses.translation: Upon the utterance of that curse, which shone like a blazing fire, divine drums resounded, a shower of flowers fell from the sky, and all the gods, led by Brahma, were greatly delighted.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञात्वा लोकगतिं सर्वां तस्य मृत्युं च रक्षसः । ऋषयः पितरश्चैव प्रीतिमापुरनुत्तमाम् ।। 7.26.59 ।।
verses.translation: Understanding the entire course of the world and the demon's impending death, the sages and ancestors indeed experienced unsurpassed joy.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु स दशग्रीवस्तं शापं रोमहर्षणम् । नारीषु मैथुने भावं नाकामास्वभ्यरोचयत् ।। 7.26.60 ।।
verses.translation: But having heard that terrifying, hair-raising curse, Dashagriva no longer desired sexual intimacy with unwilling women.

verses.sanskrit: तेन नीताः स्त्रियः प्रीतिमापुः सर्वाः पतिव्रताः । नलकूबरनिर्मुक्तं शापं श्रुत्वा मनःप्रियम् ।। 7.26.61 ।।
verses.translation: All the chaste women who had been abducted by him attained great joy upon hearing that delightful curse uttered by Nalakubara. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे षड्वविंशः सर्गः ।। 26 ।। Thus ends the twenty-sixth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
